source_textbook.name: Gilbert Strang: Introduction to Linear Algebra (18.06)

extracted_concepts.name: vector space
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A set closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication

extracted_concepts.name: basis
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A linearly independent spanning set for a vector space

extracted_concepts.name: dimension
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The number of vectors in any basis

extracted_concepts.name: span
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Set of all linear combinations of a set of vectors

extracted_concepts.name: column space
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Span of the columns of a matrix

extracted_concepts.name: null space
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Set of all x such that Ax = 0

extracted_concepts.name: rank
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Dimension of column space = dimension of row space

extracted_relations.evidence: A basis completely characterizes a vector space
Dimension is the size of any basis
A basis must be linearly independent
A basis must span the space
Column space is a subspace of Rᵐ
Null space is a subspace of Rⁿ
Rank equals dimension of column space
The theorem relates rank and nullity
The theorem relates rank and nullity
